chunk_summary: Guidance on diagnosing and rapidly correcting complementary feeding failure modes in children aged 6–24 months, focusing on energy-dense foods, structured feeding, dietary diversity, responsive feeding, and illness recovery strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the five-variable check for diagnosing complementary feeding failure modes.
Learn age-specific meal frequency and dietary recommendations for children aged 6–24 months.
Recognize the importance of responsive feeding and its impact on feeding difficulties.
Implement illness recovery feeding strategies to support growth and recovery.
key_facts: Complementary feeding should start at 6 months with energy-dense home foods, avoiding thin gruels.
Recommended meal frequency: 6–8 months: 2–3 meals/day; 9–23 months: 3–4 meals/day plus 1–2 snacks.
Prescribe thickening foods like ghee, nut pastes, or animal-source foods to improve energy density.
Responsive feeding (avoiding force-feeding or punishment) reduces feeding refusal.
During illness, continue feeding/breastfeeding and add an extra meal/snack daily for 1–2 weeks post-recovery.
reference_quote: "Start complementary feeding at 6 completed months while continuing breastfeeding, and aim for thick, energy-dense home foods rather than thin diluted gruels." (WHO, 2023)
